key_facts: Belgium 2-2 Senegal
England 2-1 DR Congo
Mexico 2-0 Ecuador
France 3-0 Sweden

stories.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Full Match Schedule and Timings for 2 July in India Time
stories.what: The FIFA World Cup 2026, being held across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, continues on 2 July with a slate of matches that Indian fans can follow through the night into the early hours. The Economic Times has published the complete fixture list for the day, including match timings converted to Indian Standard Time, venues, and the latest updates. The expanded 48-team tournament — the first of its kind — has been delivering drama throughout the group and knockout stages, drawing massive viewership globally. Indian football fans following the world's biggest sporting event can plan their viewing around the published schedule.
stories.lens: With the World Cup at its most intense knockout stage, knowing the exact IST timings helps Indian fans plan their late-night viewing without missing a moment.
stories.article:
The FIFA World Cup 2026 rolls on with another set of high-stakes fixtures on 2 July, and Indian fans tracking the action across North America have a packed night of football to look forward to.

The tournament, spread across host cities in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, is the first World Cup to feature 48 teams — a significant expansion from the traditional 32-team format. That change has meant more matches, more nations, and a longer group stage before the knockout rounds intensify. By July, the competition is into its decisive phase, with nations fighting to stay alive in the tournament.

The Economic Times has compiled the full fixture list for 2 July with timings converted to Indian Standard Time, allowing fans to track exactly when each match kicks off across the various venues. Given that most matches in North America are scheduled in afternoon or evening local time, they typically fall in the late night to early morning window for Indian viewers.

For a country where football fandom — especially around the World Cup — has grown sharply over the past decade, the schedule serves as a practical guide to one of the most-watched sporting events of the year. Streaming platforms and broadcasters have made it easier than ever for Indian audiences to follow live, making IST match timings an essential piece of information during the tournament's peak weeks.
key_facts: FIFA World Cup 2026 hosted across USA, Canada, and Mexico
First World Cup to feature 48 teams
Full 2 July fixture schedule with IST timings published
Tournament in knockout/advanced stage by July

stories.headline: Rain Ruins India's Promising Start as First T20I vs England Abandoned at Chester-le-Street
stories.what: The opening T20I between England and India at Chester-le-Street was washed out without a result after rain intervened, denying fans what had promised to be an exciting finish. Shreyas Iyer and Abhishek Sharma had both scored fifties to set India up well before the weather had the final say. The match at the Riverside Ground in Durham was abandoned, leaving the five-match series level with four games still to play. India's strong batting display, which had given them a competitive position, ultimately went unrewarded.
stories.lens: With four T20Is still to come, India's batsmen have served early notice of intent — but the weather cost them a potential series lead on the first night of the tour.
stories.article:
India's first T20 international of their England tour ended in frustration on Wednesday night as persistent rain forced the abandonment of the opening match at Chester-le-Street, with no result possible.

Before the weather intervened, India had given their fans plenty to cheer. Shreyas Iyer and Abhishek Sharma both struck half-centuries, laying the foundation for what appeared to be a competitive total. The partnership and the quality of strokeplay suggested India had arrived in England in sharp batting form, despite the unsettled English summer conditions.

The abandonment meant neither side picked up points from the first fixture, leaving the five-match series finely poised from the very first evening. For India, it was a case of what might have been — a strong batting performance yielding nothing in the standings. For England, the rain provided a lucky reprieve from a chase that could have tested their top order.

Noticeable by his absence from the playing eleven was teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, whose omission had been widely discussed in the build-up. India's management opted to retain their settled top-three order, a decision that appeared justified by the fifties from Iyer and Abhishek. The series now moves forward with the second T20I offering both sides the first real chance to gain an advantage.
key_facts: Match at Chester-le-Street abandoned due to rain
Shreyas Iyer and Abhishek Sharma both scored fifties
No result; five-match T20I series remains level
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi was not included in India's playing XI

stories.headline: Complacency and conditions blamed as India suffer whitewash
stories.what: India have been whitewashed in their most recent cricket series, with analysis pointing to a combination of complacency and challenging playing conditions as the key factors behind the sweep. The result marks a significant setback for the side, raising questions about preparation, mindset, and the team's ability to adapt when conditions are not in their favour. The defeat will intensify scrutiny on team management and selection ahead of upcoming fixtures.
stories.lens: A whitewash is rare for India and always triggers serious debate about selection and team culture — the fallout will shape squad decisions for months.
stories.article:
India have been whitewashed in their latest cricket series, a result that Hindustan Times attributes to a damaging combination of complacency and unfavourable conditions — a diagnosis that will sting the team's management and selectors alike.

The sweep represents one of the most complete defeats an Indian cricket side can suffer, and the post-mortem has been swift. Critics have pointed to a failure to treat the opposition with adequate respect, with the 'complacency' tag suggesting the team entered matches without the necessary edge or preparation intensity.

Conditions also played a significant role, according to the analysis. Indian sides have historically struggled when pitches and weather patterns diverge sharply from subcontinental norms, and this series appears to have exposed those vulnerabilities once again. The inability to adapt quickly enough proved costly across the entire series rather than in isolated moments.

The whitewash will have immediate consequences. Selectors will face hard questions about squad composition, individual form, and whether the coaching staff adequately prepared the players for what they encountered. With a busy international schedule ahead, the time to reflect is short and the pressure to respond is high.
key_facts: India suffered a whitewash in their recent cricket series
Complacency and conditions cited as key factors
Result raises questions over selection and team preparation

stories.headline: India vs England 1st T20I: Varun Chakravarthy Returns, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Focus as Weather Looms
stories.what: India face England in the first T20I of their series, with team selection generating significant buzz ahead of the match. Wrist-spinner Varun Chakravarthy is confirmed to return, giving India a major boost, while teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential inclusion is the talking point among fans and analysts. Adding uncertainty to the occasion, the weather forecast has painted a grim picture with rain threatening to disrupt play. The match has been keenly anticipated as a first major bilateral T20I assignment for several players following a busy international summer.
stories.lens: With Varun back and Sooryavanshi possibly on debut, this first T20I is a genuine selection story — and rain could deny fans the chance to see it unfold.
stories.article:
India's opening T20I against England has the makings of an enthralling contest, but it is the team selection drama and the looming weather threat that are dominating the build-up.

Varun Chakravarthy's return to the Indian T20I setup is the headline selection news, handing India a potent spinning option. The mystery spinner has been among the most effective T20I bowlers in his international stints, and his availability gives India's attack a sharper edge against England's batting line-up.

The bigger talking point, however, is whether Vaibhav Sooryavanshi — the teenage prodigy who has set domestic cricket alight — will be handed his chance. Selectors and team management face a genuine dilemma over whether to blood the youngster in this series opener, and the debate has taken on a life of its own in cricketing circles ahead of the match.

Overshadowing both storylines is a grim weather forecast, with rain threatening to curtail or even wash out proceedings. For fans who have been eagerly waiting to see this Indian side take the field in T20I cricket, the possibility of a weather interruption is a frustrating cloud hanging over what promises to be an exciting contest. The match is available for fans to watch via live streaming and telecast across official broadcast platforms.
key_facts: Varun Chakravarthy returns to boost India's T20I bowling attack
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's possible inclusion is the key selection debate
Rain forecast threatens to disrupt the 1st T20I
India vs England is the marquee T20I series opener

stories.headline: Ishan Kishan Rises to World No. 1 in ICC T20I Batting Rankings, Displacing Abhishek Sharma
stories.what: Ishan Kishan has climbed to the top of the ICC T20I batting rankings, replacing fellow Indian opener Abhishek Sharma as the world's number-one ranked batter in the format. The resurgence is a remarkable turnaround for Kishan, who had been out of the national team setup for an extended period. The new rankings reflect his strong recent performances that have propelled him ahead of his compatriot. It is a notable moment for Indian cricket, with two Indian openers occupying the summit of the global T20I batting charts.
stories.lens: Two Indians sitting at the very top of the ICC T20I batting rankings underlines India's dominance in the shortest international format right now.
stories.article:
Ishan Kishan has stormed to the top of the ICC T20I batting rankings, displacing Abhishek Sharma to become the world's number-one ranked batter in the format — a striking statement of his return to form at the international level.

The development marks a significant personal milestone for Kishan, whose international career had stalled after he was dropped from the Indian setup following a period of controversy over his non-availability. His climb to the summit of the global rankings signals that his bat has done the talking on his return to competitive cricket.

Equally notable is that Abhishek Sharma, who Kishan displaces, is also an Indian. The two openers sitting at the very top of the ICC T20I batting charts reflects the extraordinary depth and quality of India's batting resources in the format, with selectors spoiled for choice at the top of the order.

The ICC rankings update comes at a timely moment as India are preparing for their T20I series against England. Kishan's ascent to world number one will only add to the selection debates and the pressure on the team management to field their best possible combination.
key_facts: Ishan Kishan is now world No. 1 in ICC T20I batting rankings
He displaces fellow Indian Abhishek Sharma from the top spot
Two Indians now occupy the pinnacle of T20I batting rankings

stories.headline: Aamir Khan to attend IFFM curtain raiser in Melbourne as Lagaan completes 25 years
stories.what: Aamir Khan is set to attend the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne's curtain raiser event as his landmark film Lagaan marks its 25th anniversary. The milestone puts a spotlight on one of Indian cinema's most celebrated films, which earned an Academy Award nomination and remains a touchstone of Hindi filmmaking. Khan's Melbourne appearance signals a strong international push for the anniversary celebrations.
stories.lens: Lagaan's 25-year milestone is a reminder of how a single film can carry Indian cinema to global stages — and Aamir Khan's Melbourne visit keeps that legacy alive for a new generation.
stories.article:
Aamir Khan will travel to Melbourne to attend the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne's (IFFM) curtain raiser event, marking a significant personal and cinematic occasion as his iconic film Lagaan completes 25 years.

Lagaan, released in 2001, remains one of the most celebrated films in Hindi cinema history. The period sports drama earned India an Academy Award nomination for Best Foreign Language Film, one of only a handful of Bollywood productions to reach that stage, and its blend of cricket, colonialism and music made it a cultural phenomenon both domestically and internationally.

Khan's decision to attend the IFFM event underscores the continuing global resonance of the film and its enduring place in the Indian diaspora's cultural memory. The Indian Film Festival of Melbourne is one of the largest celebrations of Indian cinema outside India, and a 25th-anniversary spotlight on Lagaan is expected to draw significant attention from both film enthusiasts and the Indian-Australian community.

The appearance also keeps Khan in the international spotlight at a time when Indian cinema is actively courting global audiences and festival circuits. For fans, the Melbourne event represents a rare opportunity to celebrate a generational classic with the actor who brought it to life.
key_facts: Aamir Khan attending IFFM curtain raiser in Melbourne
Lagaan completing its 25th anniversary
Lagaan received an Academy Award nomination for Best Foreign Language Film

stories.headline: Neha Dhupia loses temper at paparazzi over 'disrespectful' back shots
stories.what: Actor Neha Dhupia publicly lost her temper at paparazzi photographers, objecting to what she described as 'disrespectful' back shots taken without her consent or consideration. The confrontation highlights a growing tension between celebrities and the paparazzi culture that has exploded in India in the social-media era. Dhupia's pushback adds her voice to a broader conversation about where the line falls between public interest and personal dignity.
stories.lens: Dhupia's confrontation with photographers spotlights the rarely discussed flip side of India's booming paparazzi culture — the boundary between a celebrity's public persona and their right to be photographed with dignity.
stories.article:
Neha Dhupia made headlines after openly confronting paparazzi photographers, calling out what she termed 'disrespectful' back shots taken of her — photographs she clearly felt crossed a line of basic dignity.

The incident, reported by The Indian Express, reflects a tension that has steadily grown as Mumbai's celebrity paparazzi culture has expanded dramatically over the past decade, fuelled by social media demand for candid celebrity content. While many actors have quietly accepted constant camera surveillance as a cost of fame, Dhupia's direct and vocal response was notable for its forcefulness.

Her objection to back shots — images often taken without the subject's awareness, focusing on their body in ways they cannot anticipate or consent to — touches on a specific and sensitive dimension of celebrity photography. Critics of such practices argue that these images are frequently taken with the explicit purpose of generating content that the subject themselves would find embarrassing or objectifying.

Dhupia's reaction is likely to resonate with audiences who have increasingly called for more respectful coverage of public figures, particularly women. Whether it prompts any meaningful change in how photographers approach celebrities remains to be seen, but the confrontation has amplified a conversation about media ethics that the Indian entertainment industry has long sidestepped.
key_facts: Neha Dhupia confronted paparazzi photographers publicly
Objected to 'disrespectful' back shots
Incident reported by The Indian Express

stories.headline: Abhishek Bachchan recalls battling insecurity in Bollywood: 'Kaam milega ya nahi'
stories.what: Abhishek Bachchan has opened up about the deep-seated insecurity he faced during his career in Bollywood, summarising the anxiety with the candid phrase 'Kaam milega ya nahi' — will I get work or not. Speaking with unusual frankness, the actor shed light on the psychological pressures of sustaining a career in Hindi cinema, even as the son of a legendary star. His reflection offers a rare, unguarded glimpse into the vulnerability experienced by even established industry figures.
stories.lens: Abhishek Bachchan's candid admission that even a star son wrestled with the fear of not getting work speaks to the precarious, merit-defying nature of Bollywood success that millions of aspiring actors grapple with.
stories.article:
Abhishek Bachchan has spoken candidly about the career insecurities that plagued him during his time in Bollywood, admitting that the constant fear of whether work would come defined a significant part of his professional journey.

In remarks reported by The Indian Express, Bachchan encapsulated that anxiety in a plain, relatable phrase — 'Kaam milega ya nahi' (will I get work or not) — a sentiment that resonates far beyond star corridors and into the everyday reality of thousands who chase dreams in India's film industry. Despite carrying one of Hindi cinema's most recognisable surnames, Bachchan acknowledged that lineage alone provides no insulation from professional doubt.

The confession is striking given that Bollywood's star-kid debate has intensified in public discourse in recent years, with audiences and industry insiders frequently discussing whether nepotism paves an easier path. Bachchan's account complicates that narrative, suggesting that even those born into privilege navigate their own versions of uncertainty and self-doubt.

His remarks are likely to resonate with audiences who have watched his career arc — from celebrated early roles to a period of diminished visibility, and a recent return to prominence. The actor's willingness to speak about vulnerability, rather than project invulnerability, marks a notable shift in how Bollywood's second generation is choosing to engage with the public conversation about their place in the industry.
key_facts: Abhishek Bachchan spoke about career insecurity in Bollywood
Described anxiety with phrase 'Kaam milega ya nahi' (will I get work or not)
Remarks reported by The Indian Express

stories.headline: Veteran Theatre and Film Director Vijaya Mehta Dies at 91
stories.what: Vijaya Mehta, one of India's most celebrated theatre and film directors, passed away at the age of 91. A towering figure in Marathi theatre and Indian cinema, she shaped generations of performers and playwrights through her decades-long career. Her death marks the end of an extraordinary era in Indian performing arts. The news was reported late on Wednesday night.
stories.lens: Vijaya Mehta's passing closes a chapter of Indian theatre history — her work defined Marathi stage culture and mentored many of the country's finest actors and directors.
stories.article:
Vijaya Mehta, the legendary theatre and film director who stood at the heart of Indian performing arts for over six decades, died at the age of 91. Her passing leaves an immense void in Marathi theatre and the broader Indian cultural landscape, where she was revered as a visionary craftsperson and institution-builder.

Mehta's contributions to Marathi theatre were foundational. As a director, actor, and teacher, she shaped the idiom of modern Marathi stage, bringing rigour, innovation and a deep humanist sensibility to productions that influenced generations of artists. She was associated with landmark institutions and counted some of India's most distinguished performers among her students and collaborators.

Her work extended to film and television, where she brought the same meticulous attention to character and narrative that defined her stage productions. Awards and recognition followed her across her career, but those who worked with her consistently spoke of her gift for nurturing talent and her uncompromising artistic standards.

The Indian cultural community has been paying tribute since the news broke, reflecting the breadth of her influence across languages, mediums and generations. Vijaya Mehta was 91.
key_facts: Vijaya Mehta, veteran theatre and film director, passed away
She was 91 years old
A towering figure in Marathi theatre and Indian cinema

stories.headline: Jaya Janaki Nayaka Becomes Highest-Watched Indian Film on YouTube; Rakul Preet Singh Celebrates
stories.what: Telugu film Jaya Janaki Nayaka has achieved the landmark of becoming the highest-watched Indian film on YouTube, a milestone that actress Rakul Preet Singh — one of its stars — took to social media to celebrate. The 2017 romantic action film, featuring Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh, has now surpassed all rivals on the platform in terms of viewership. The achievement underlines the extraordinary reach of Telugu cinema on digital platforms.
stories.lens: Jaya Janaki Nayaka's YouTube record is a vivid reminder of how Telugu cinema has built a massive global digital audience — and how platforms like YouTube are reshaping which films endure in popular memory.
stories.article:
Jaya Janaki Nayaka has been crowned the highest-watched Indian film on YouTube, a record milestone that actress Rakul Preet Singh celebrated publicly, marking a remarkable achievement for the Telugu film industry's digital footprint.

The 2017 romantic action entertainer, which starred Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh, has accumulated viewership on YouTube that surpasses every other Indian film on the platform — a testament to the enduring popularity of Telugu mass entertainers with diaspora and domestic audiences alike. Rakul shared her excitement on social media, calling attention to what the record represents for the film and its team.

The milestone reflects a broader trend: Telugu and other South Indian films have consistently outperformed Bollywood productions on streaming and digital platforms, drawing on enormous fan bases in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and among the global Telugu-speaking community. YouTube's free-access model has made it a particularly powerful vehicle for this reach.

For Rakul Preet Singh, who has since built a prominent cross-industry career in both Telugu and Hindi films, the record is a nostalgic marker of one of her most beloved earlier performances. The news has sparked a wave of celebration among the film's fans online.
key_facts: Jaya Janaki Nayaka named highest-watched Indian film on YouTube
Film starred Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas and Rakul Preet Singh (2017)
Rakul Preet Singh celebrated the milestone on social media
